title: Codebase Agent-Readiness Rubric
description: Machine-readable scoring rubric for how ready a repository is for agents to work in it effectively and verifiably. Consumed by the aios-workspace validator (OGR10) and the aios-team-brain codebase scan. Each check is a binary, automatable signal (file existence + lightweight config parse). A repo's level is the highest level for which >= advanceThreshold of that level's cumulative checks pass. The Spine level is CAPPED at L3 when the Verification pillar scores 0 passing checks (the core AEM rule: no real agentic maturity without verification).

levels.name: Functional
levels.blurb: A human can build and run it.

levels.name: Agent-ready
levels.blurb: An agent can work here safely and verifiably.

levels.name: Self-improving
levels.blurb: The repo helps agents improve it.

checks.title: README exists
checks.rationale: Baseline orientation for any worker, human or agent.

checks.title: Declares how to build/run (package manifest)
checks.rationale: A worker must be able to install and run the project.

checks.title: CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md exists
checks.rationale: Agents need a conventions/gotchas file to work effectively (the highest-leverage agent-readiness signal).

checks.title: Agent instructions are non-trivial (>= 400 bytes)
checks.rationale: A stub CLAUDE.md doesn't help; substance does.

checks.title: Linter configured
checks.rationale: Static guardrail that keeps agent output consistent.

checks.title: Formatter configured
checks.rationale: Deterministic formatting handles the 'last 10%' agents miss.

checks.title: A test suite exists
checks.rationale: The agent's primary feedback loop; verification is the AEM differentiator.

checks.title: CI runs on PRs
checks.rationale: Automates the verification loop so agent changes are checked before merge.

checks.title: Type-checker configured
checks.rationale: Types are a strong guardrail that catches a class of agent mistakes at edit time.

checks.title: One-command setup / dev environment
checks.rationale: Agents (and humans) get a reproducible environment without guesswork.

checks.title: Setup/contributing docs present
checks.rationale: Documents how to work in the repo beyond the README stub.

checks.title: Secret scanning / ignore hygiene
checks.rationale: Prevents agents from committing or surfacing secrets.

checks.title: Pre-commit / pre-push hooks
checks.rationale: Enforces quality gates deterministically, not by reminder.

checks.title: Logging / monitoring present
checks.rationale: Agent actions and runtime behavior are inspectable.

checks.title: Eval suite or LLM-as-judge harness
checks.rationale: Verification becomes a first-class, measured artifact (EDD).

checks.title: Agent instructions show compounding (substantial, sectioned)
checks.rationale: A living, frequently-updated CLAUDE.md that has grown past a single page is a signal of compounding engineering.

checks.title: Shared agent skills / commands
checks.rationale: The repo ships reusable agent harnesses (skills, slash-commands), not just prose.

checks.title: Agent-readable docs (llms.txt)
checks.rationale: Built for agents as a first-class consumer of the repo's information.
